revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2018年8月}} {{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 59.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 03.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Wakayama | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[大己貴命]]
[[事代主命]]ら他8神 |社格 = |創建 = 1836年（天保7年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |例祭 = [[4月10日]] |神事 = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中の神社。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] 他8神　 == 歴史 == 社殿によると創建は1836年（天保7年）に、讃岐国の[[金刀比羅宮]]より地域住民が勧請したとされている。明治時代に入り[[神社合祀]]運動により近隣の郷社・日神社に合祀されたが、地域住民が再三にわたり復社の要望を郡役所に出した結果、1911年（明治44年）に和歌山県庁より復社が認められた[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ 金刀比羅神社 | 南方熊楠顕彰館（南方熊楠邸）– Minakata Kumagusu Archives]。 [[南方熊楠]]は、『神社合祀に関する意見』において復社の経緯を記しており、合祀先神社の神職が祭祀の兼職を維持しようと金刀比羅神社のご神体を住民に返さず隠していた。しかし、合祀先神社に渡していたご神体は偽者であり、合祀の際に住民が隠していた本物のご神体によって神社の復社を完了したという逸話が残されている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄バス停：JR[[紀勢本線]][[紀伊富田駅]]下車後、徒歩約18分（南西に約1.4km） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 金刀比羅神社 - 和歌山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{南方曼陀羅の風景地}} {{デフォルトソート:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:白浜町の建築物]] [[Category:白浜町の地理]]

revision.text:
金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = {{画像募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | Location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | Coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama Town|ja|白浜町}}, Nishimuro District, Wakayama Prefecture.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; Location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 Kotohira Shrine - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association]

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = {{画像募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | Location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | Coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; Location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | Coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 Kotohira Shrine - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association]

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities

During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>

He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 Kotohira Shrine - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association]

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]

[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 Kotohira Shrine - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association]

; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)

[[Category:kk]]
[[Category:kk]]

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: categories]]
<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.

== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Shirahama)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|ja|白浜町}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}. During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

During the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|12=draft}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|ja|南方熊楠|12=draft}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|ja|紀勢本線|12=draft}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|ja|紀伊富田駅|12=draft}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2018年8月}} {{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=西牟婁郡}} |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 59.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 03.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Wakayama | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[大己貴命]]
[[事代主命]]ら他8神 |社格 = |創建 = 1836年（天保7年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |例祭 = [[4月10日]] |神事 = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中の神社。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] 他8神　 == 歴史 == 社殿によると創建は1836年（天保7年）に、讃岐国の[[金刀比羅宮]]より地域住民が勧請したとされている。明治時代に入り[[神社合祀]]運動により近隣の郷社・日神社に合祀されたが、地域住民が再三にわたり復社の要望を郡役所に出した結果、1911年（明治44年）に和歌山県庁より復社が認められた[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ 金刀比羅神社 | 南方熊楠顕彰館（南方熊楠邸）– Minakata Kumagusu Archives]。 [[南方熊楠]]は、『神社合祀に関する意見』において復社の経緯を記しており、合祀先神社の神職が祭祀の兼職を維持しようと金刀比羅神社のご神体を住民に返さず隠していた。しかし、合祀先神社に渡していたご神体は偽者であり、合祀の際に住民が隠していた本物のご神体によって神社の復社を完了したという逸話が残されている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄バス停：JR[[紀勢本線]][[紀伊富田駅]]下車後、徒歩約18分（南西に約1.4km） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 金刀比羅神社 - 和歌山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:白浜町の建築物]] [[Category:白浜町の歴史]]

{{単一の出典|date=2018年8月}} {{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=西牟婁郡}} |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 | 緯度度 = 33 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 59.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 23 | 経度秒 = 03.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Wakayama | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[大己貴命]]
[[事代主命]]ら他8神 |社格 = |創建 = 1836年（天保7年） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |例祭 = [[4月10日]] |神事 = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中の神社。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] 他8神　 == 歴史 == 社殿によると創建は1836年（天保7年）に、讃岐国の[[金刀比羅宮]]より地域住民が勧請したとされている。明治時代に入り[[神社合祀]]運動により近隣の郷社・日神社に合祀されたが、地域住民が再三にわたり復社の要望を郡役所に出した結果、1911年（明治44年）に和歌山県庁より復社が認められた[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ 金刀比羅神社 | 南方熊楠顕彰館（南方熊楠邸）– Minakata Kumagusu Archives]。 [[南方熊楠]]は、『神社合祀に関する意見』において復社の経緯を記しており、合祀先神社の神職が祭祀の兼職を維持しようと金刀比羅神社のご神体を住民に返さず隠していた。しかし、合祀先神社に渡していたご神体は偽者であり、合祀の際に住民が隠していた本物のご神体によって神社の復社を完了したという逸話が残されている。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[和歌山県]][[西牟婁郡]][[白浜町]]中字鳥之倉1702番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄バス停：JR[[紀勢本線]][[紀伊富田駅]]下車後、徒歩約18分（南西に約1.4km） == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 金刀比羅神社 - 和歌山県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:和歌山県の神社]] [[Category:白浜町の建築物]] [[Category:白浜町の歴史]]

revision.text:
金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-shiro-nushi|qq=}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama, Wakayama]].
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Kii-Tonda Station|Kii-Tonda Station]], followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=7021 Kotohira Shrine - Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Association]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Minakata Mandala Scenic Spot}}
{{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)|version=103229145|comment=Imported full ja history}}

金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-shiro-nushi|qq=}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama, Wakayama]].
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Kii-Tonda Station|Kii-Tonda Station]], followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama, Wakayama]].

== Deities ==
; Main Deities
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
* 8 other deities

== History ==
According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>

[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama, Wakayama]].

== Deities ==
; Main Deities
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
* 8 other deities

== History ==
According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>

[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

== Information ==
; location
* Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702

; Access
* Nearest bus stop: JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Kii-Tonda Station|Kii-Tonda Station]], followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama]], [[:en:Nishimuro District|Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-shiro-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shirahama, Wakayama|Shirahama, Wakayama]].
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Kii-Tonda Station|Kii-Tonda Station]], followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.

== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] from [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]]. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
[[:en:Minakata Kumagusu|Minakata Kumagusu]] documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR [[:en:Kisei Main Line|Kisei Main Line]] [[:en:Kii-Tonda Station|Kii-Tonda Station]], followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (白浜町)]]
金刀比羅神社_(白浜町)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama}}, [[:en:Nishimuro District|Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-shiro-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama, Wakayama}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|en|Minakata Kumagusu|WD=Q6862791|lt=Minakata Kumagusu}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|en|Kii-Tonda Station|WD=Q2888483|lt=Kii-Tonda Station}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Nishimuro District}} | location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, [[:en:Nishimuro District|Nishimuro District]], [[Shirahama Town]], Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702 | coordinates = {{coord|33|38|59.5|N|135|23|03.2|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Koto-shiro-nushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koto-shiro-nushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8009611}}, and 8 other deities | Classification = | Established = 1836 (Tenpō 7) | MainHall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = April 10 | Rituals = }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=260}}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Shirahama, Wakayama|en|Shirahama, Wakayama|WD=Q1202728|lt=Shirahama, Wakayama|1=Shirahama, Wakayama}}.
== Deities == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * 8 other deities
== History == According to shrine records, the shrine was established in 1836 (Tenpō 7) when local residents invited the deity of {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}} from {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with the nearby gōsha, Nissha Shrine, as part of the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|en|Shrine Consolidation Policy|WD=Q11590700|lt=Shrine Consolidation Policy|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. However, due to persistent requests from local residents, the shrine was reinstated in 1911 (Meiji 44) with approval from the Wakayama Prefectural Office.<ref>[http://www.minakata.org/relatedspot/minakatamandalanofukeichi/kotohirajinjya/ Kotohira Shrine | Minakata Kumagusu Archives]</ref>
{{ill|Minakata Kumagusu|en|Minakata Kumagusu|WD=Q6862791|lt=Minakata Kumagusu|1=Minakata Kumagusu}} documented the reinstatement process in his "Opinion on Shrine Mergers." He noted that the priest of the merged shrine attempted to retain the Kotohira Shrine's deity, hiding the sacred object from the residents. However, the object given to the merged shrine was a counterfeit, and the true sacred object, secretly kept by the residents, facilitated the shrine's restoration. This anecdote remains a significant part of the shrine's history.
== Information == ; location * Wakayama Prefecture, Nishimuro District, Shirahama Town, Chūaza Tori-no-Kura 1702
; Access * Nearest bus stop: JR {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|WD=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|Kii-Tonda Station|en|Kii-Tonda Station|WD=Q2888483|lt=Kii-Tonda Station|1=Kii-Tonda Station}}, followed by an 18-minute walk southwest (approximately 1.4 km).
